tools.name: searchDocumentation
tools.description: Search across the documentation to find relevant information, code examples, API references, and guides. Use this tool when you need to answer questions about BlockRazor, find specific documentation, understand how features work, or locate implementation details. The search returns contextual conten

tools.name: getPage
tools.description: Fetch the full markdown content of a specific documentation page from BlockRazor. Use this when you have a page URL and want to read its content. Accepts full URLs (e.g. https://docs.blockrazor.io//getting-started). Since `searchDocumentation` returns partial content, use `getPage` to retrieve the c

tools.name: askQuestion
tools.description: Ask a natural-language question about BlockRazor and get a synthesized answer, with links to the source pages. Prefer this over `searchDocumentation` when you want a direct answer to a question rather than a list of matching pages; use `searchDocumentation`/`getPage` when you need to browse or read

tools.name: sendFeedback
tools.description: Report an issue in the documentation of BlockRazor so the team can fix it. Use it whenever, while helping a user, you come across content that is outdated, contradictory, missing information, or otherwise unhelpful. Also use it when the user themselves reports a problem with the docs, even if you co

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "docs" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: getPage, sendFeedback · Public sink: sendFeedback.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: getPage, sendFeedback · Public sink: sendFeedback

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
